entryA.symptom: Content Trust signing fails when using ACR admin account as signer - permission error during docker
entryA.solution: Use a Service Principal with AcrImageSigner and AcrPush RBAC roles instead of admin account. Grant signer permission as per: https://docs.microsoft.co

entryB.symptom: Docker Content Trust (DCT) is being deprecated in ACR; new registries cannot enable DCT after 2026-0
entryB.solution: 1) Migrate to Notary Project-based signing with Azure Key Vault: https://docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/container-registry/container-registry-tutorial-sign-build

conflicts.reasoning: ado-wiki-009 describes DCT signing with admin account (still valid for existing DCT users), while onenote-009 describes DCT deprecation and migration to Notation. Both are valid: one for legacy users, one for new adoption.
conflicts.recommendation: Both entries retained. Guide should note DCT is deprecated (per onenote-009) and recommend Notation for new implementations, while keeping DCT troubleshooting for legacy users.

entryA.solution: 1) Retrieve AzureContainerRegistry service tag IPs for your region: $serviceTags = Get-AzNetworkServiceTag -Location $region; ($serviceTags.Values | W

entryB.symptom: az acr build fails with "denied: client with IP X.X.X.X is not allowed access" when ACR has public n
entryB.solution: Option A (recommended, strict private-only): Create an ACR Dedicated Agent Pool injected into the VNet: az acr agentpool create --registry <acrName> -

conflicts.reasoning: Both describe ACR build failing with IP not allowed. onenote-003 focuses on service tag and firewall rules; ado-wiki-a-r1-001 focuses on private endpoint with public network disabled. Different network configs, different solutions.
conflicts.recommendation: Both retained. Guide should present as different network scenarios with distinct troubleshooting paths.

entryA.symptom: ACR Task fails to run when the registry has public access disabled (network-restricted ACR)
entryA.solution: 1) Enable 'Allow trusted Microsoft services to access this container registry' on the ACR. 2) Create ACR task with --assign-identity (system-assigned

entryB.symptom: ACR Tasks fail with 403 Forbidden errors after June 2025 — tasks that previously worked with network
entryB.solution: 1) Explicitly enable the new flag: az acr update --name <registry-name> --set networkRuleBypassAllowedForTasks=true. 2) Alternatively, use ACR Agent P

conflicts.reasoning: onenote-002 is general guidance for Tasks on network-restricted ACR (use managed identity + trusted service). ado-wiki-041 describes a specific June 2025 security change that broke existing Tasks. Both are valid but ado-wiki-041 has newer context about the security change.
conflicts.recommendation: Both retained. ado-wiki-041 should be presented first as it includes the latest security change context. onenote-002 provides the general solution pattern.
